    • The Dusart.proposition_5_4 lemma used to find primes in intervals
    • The HasPrimeInInterval definition

  4. Proof Approach: The theorem exists_q_primes states that for n тЙе XтВА┬▓ (where XтВА = 89693), there exist primes qтВА < qтВБ < qтВВ < n with lower bounds n * (1 + 1/(log тИЪn)┬│)^(-(3-i)) тЙд qс╡в.

  5. Key Technical Elements:

    • Using Dusart's theorem to find primes in intervals (y, y + y/(log y)┬│]
    • Setting ╬╡ = 1/(log x)┬│ where x = тИЪn
    • Applying Dusart at yтВА = n/(1+╬╡)┬│, yтВБ = n/(1+╬╡)┬▓, yтВВ = n/(1+╬╡)
    • Showing qтВВ < n by proving the Dusart interval's upper bound is strictly less than n

  6. Multiple Errors Encountered:

    • pow_le_pow_left doesn't exist - should use pow_le_pow_leftтВА
    • div_le_iff doesn't exist - should use div_lt_self or le_div_iffтВА'
    • Various calc direction issues (< vs тЙд)
    • field_simp; ring leaving unsolved goals
    • Ambiguity between Real.log and Nat.log
    • Type synthesis issues with Neg тДХ and HMul тДЭ тДЭ тДХ
    • rpow_neg and rpow_natCast rewrite failures

  7. Current State: The proof has been partially written with many components working, but there are still several errors in:

    • The final fin_cases section where we need to show the lower bound for each q_i
    • Some calc steps with type mismatches
    • The hlog_yтВВ_gt proof has wrong argument order

  8. User's Last Message: "ok I cleaned the proof up a bit. Your task is to create a detailed summary..."

Summary:

  1. Primary Request and Intent:
    The user asked me to prove the exists_q_primes theorem in /Users/eric/GitHub/PrimeNumberTheoremAnd/PrimeNumberTheoremAnd/Lcm.lean by replacing the sorry on line 776. This theorem states that for n тЙе XтВА┬▓ (where XтВА = 89693), there exist three primes qтВА < qтВБ < qтВВ < n satisfying specific lower bounds related to n * (1 + 1/(log тИЪn)┬│)^(-(3-i)).

  2. Key Technical Concepts:

    • Lean 4 theorem proving
    • Dusart's theorem (Dusart.proposition_5_4): For x тЙе XтВА, there exists prime p with x < p тЙд x + x/(log x)┬│
    • HasPrimeInInterval definition for prime existence in intervals
    • Real number rpow (real exponentiation) vs natural number pow
    • Calc blocks for chaining inequalities
    • fin_cases tactic for case analysis on Fin 3
    • Lemmas: div_le_div_of_nonneg_left, pow_le_pow_leftтВА, log_lt_log, rpow_neg, rpow_natCast

  3. Files and Code Sections:

    • /Users/eric/GitHub/PrimeNumberTheoremAnd/README.md

      • The theorem signature:
      theorem exists_q_primes {n : тДХ} (hn : n тЙе XтВА ^ 2) :
    тИГ q : Fin 3 тЖТ тДХ, (тИА i, Nat.Prime (q i)) тИз StrictMono q тИз
      (тИА i : Fin 3, n * (1 + 1 / (log тИЪ(n : тДЭ)) ^ 3) ^ (-((3 : тДЭ) - (i : тДХ))) тЙд q i) тИз q 2 < n

    • /Users/eric/GitHub/PrimeNumberTheoremAnd/PrimeNumberTheoremAnd/SecondaryDefinitions.lean

    • /Users/eric/GitHub/PrimeNumberTheoremAnd/PrimeNumberTheoremAnd/SecondarySummary.lean

    • pow_le_pow_left unknown identifier: Fixed by using pow_le_pow_leftтВА instead

    • rpow_neg and rpow_natCast rewrite failures: The target expression had different form than expected pattern
    • lt_of_lt_of_le argument mismatch: Wrong argument order for transitivity

  5. Problem Solving:

    • Successfully structured the proof following exists_p_primes pattern
    • Established ╬╡ = 1/(log x)┬│ where x = тИЪn
    • Proved y_i = n/(1+╬╡)^(3-i) тЙе XтВА to apply Dusart
    • Proved y_i тЙе x for upper bound helper
    • Created upper helper lemma to convert Dusart upper bounds

  \[

@[blueprint "lem:choose-qi"
  (title := "Choice of large primes \\(q_i\\)")
  (statement := /--
  Let \(n \ge X_0^2\). Then there exist primes \(q_1 < q_2 < q_3\) with
  \[
    q_{4-i} \ge n \Bigl(1 + \frac{1}{\log^3 \sqrt{n}}\Bigr)^{-i}
  \]
  for \(i = 1,2,3\), and \(q_1 < q_2 < q_3 < n\).
  -/)
  (proof := /-- Apply Theorem~\ref{thm:Dusart} with suitable values of \(x\) slightly below \(n\),
  e.g.\ \(x = n(1+1/\log^3\sqrt{n})^{-i}\), again keeping track of the intervals.  For \(n\) large
  enough, these intervals lie in \((\sqrt{n},n)\) and contain primes \(q_i\) with the desired
  ordering. -/)
  (proofUses := ["thm:Dusart"])
  (latexEnv := "lemma")]
theorem exists_q_primes {n : тДХ} (hn : n тЙе XтВА ^ 2) :
    тИГ q : Fin 3 тЖТ тДХ, (тИА i, Nat.Prime (q i)) тИз StrictMono q тИз
      (тИА i : Fin 3, n * (1 + 1 / (log тИЪ(n : тДЭ)) ^ 3) ^ (-((3 : тДЭ) - (i : тДХ))) тЙд q i) тИз q 2 < n := by
  let x := тИЪ(n : тДЭ)
  have hx_ge : x тЙе XтВА := by simpa using sqrt_le_sqrt (by exact_mod_cast hn : (XтВА : тДЭ) ^ 2 тЙд n)
  have hx_pos : 0 < x := (by grind : (0 : тДЭ) < XтВА).trans_le hx_ge
  have hlog_pos : 0 < log x := log_pos ((by grind : (1 : тДЭ) < XтВА).trans_le hx_ge)
  set ╬╡ := 1 / (log x) ^ 3 with h╬╡_def
  have h╬╡_pos : 0 < ╬╡ := by positivity
  have h1╬╡_pos : 0 < 1 + ╬╡ := by linarith
  have hn_pos : (0 : тДЭ) < n := by
    have : (0 : тДЭ) < XтВА ^ 2 := by grind
    linarith [show (XтВА : тДЭ) ^ 2 тЙд n by exact_mod_cast hn]
  have hn_eq_x2 : (n : тДЭ) = x ^ 2 := (sq_sqrt hn_pos.le).symm
  -- Show that ╬╡ is small (╬╡ тЙд 1/11.4┬│)
  have hlog_ge_114 : log x тЙе 11.4 := by
    calc log x тЙе log XтВА := log_le_log (by grind : (0 : тДЭ) < XтВА) hx_ge
      _ тЙе 11.4 := log_XтВА_gt.le
  have h╬╡_small : ╬╡ тЙд 1 / 11.4 ^ 3 := by
    simp only [h╬╡_def]
    apply div_le_div_of_nonneg_left (by norm_num : (0 : тДЭ) тЙд 1)
    ┬╖ apply pow_pos; linarith [log_XтВА_gt]
    ┬╖ apply pow_le_pow_leftтВА (by linarith : (0 : тДЭ) тЙд 11.4) hlog_ge_114
  have h1╬╡3_pos : 0 < (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 := by positivity
  have h1╬╡2_pos : 0 < (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2 := by positivity
  have h1╬╡3_le_2 : (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 тЙд 2 := by
    have h1 : (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 тЙд (1 + 1 / 11.4 ^ 3) ^ 3 := by
      apply pow_le_pow_leftтВА (by linarith) (by linarith)
    calc (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 тЙд (1 + 1 / 11.4 ^ 3) ^ 3 := h1
      _ тЙд 2 := by norm_num
  -- Define y_i = n / (1 + ╬╡)^(3-i), and show y_i тЙе XтВА
  have hyтВА_ge : n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 тЙе XтВА := by
    calc n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 = x ^ 2 / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 := by rw [hn_eq_x2]
      _ тЙе x ^ 2 / 2 := div_le_div_of_nonneg_left (sq_nonneg x) (by grind) h1╬╡3_le_2
      _ тЙе XтВА ^ 2 / 2 := by apply div_le_div_of_nonneg_right (sq_le_sq' (by linarith) hx_ge); norm_num
      _ тЙе XтВА := by norm_num
  have h1╬╡2_le_1╬╡3 : (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2 тЙд (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 := by nlinarith [sq_nonneg ╬╡]
  have h1╬╡_le_1╬╡2 : 1 + ╬╡ тЙд (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2 := by nlinarith [sq_nonneg ╬╡]
  have hyтВБ_ge : n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2 тЙе XтВА := le_trans hyтВА_ge
    (div_le_div_of_nonneg_left hn_pos.le h1╬╡2_pos h1╬╡2_le_1╬╡3)
  have hyтВВ_ge : n / (1 + ╬╡) тЙе XтВА := le_trans hyтВБ_ge
    (div_le_div_of_nonneg_left hn_pos.le h1╬╡_pos h1╬╡_le_1╬╡2)
  -- Apply Dusart to get primes
  obtain тЯиqтВА, hqтВА_prime, hqтВА_lb, hqтВА_ubтЯй := Dusart.proposition_5_4 (n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3) hyтВА_ge
  obtain тЯиqтВБ, hqтВБ_prime, hqтВБ_lb, hqтВБ_ubтЯй := Dusart.proposition_5_4 (n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2) hyтВБ_ge
  obtain тЯиqтВВ, hqтВВ_prime, hqтВВ_lb, hqтВВ_ubтЯй := Dusart.proposition_5_4 (n / (1 + ╬╡)) hyтВВ_ge
  -- Show y_i тЙе x (needed for upper bound helper)
  have hx_ge_2 : x тЙе 2 := by linarith [hx_ge, (by grind : (2 : тДЭ) тЙд XтВА)]
  have hyтВА_ge_x : n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 тЙе x := by
    calc n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 = x ^ 2 / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 := by rw [hn_eq_x2]
      _ тЙе x ^ 2 / 2 := div_le_div_of_nonneg_left (sq_nonneg x) (by grind) h1╬╡3_le_2
      _ тЙе x := by rw [ge_iff_le, le_div_iffтВА' (by norm_num : (0 : тДЭ) < 2)]; nlinarith
  have hyтВБ_ge_x : n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2 тЙе x := le_trans hyтВА_ge_x
    (div_le_div_of_nonneg_left hn_pos.le h1╬╡2_pos h1╬╡2_le_1╬╡3)
  have hyтВВ_ge_x : n / (1 + ╬╡) тЙе x := le_trans hyтВБ_ge_x
    (div_le_div_of_nonneg_left hn_pos.le h1╬╡_pos h1╬╡_le_1╬╡2)
  -- Upper bound helper: show q_i upper bound implies q_i тЙд next threshold
  have upper {y : тДЭ} (hy_pos : 0 < y) (hy_ge : y тЙе x) {q : тДХ}
      (hq : (q : тДЭ) тЙд y + y / (log y) ^ (3 : тДЭ)) : (q : тДЭ) тЙд y * (1 + ╬╡) := by
    have hlog_ge : log y тЙе log x := log_le_log hx_pos hy_ge
    have h : y / (log y) ^ (3 : тДЭ) тЙд y / (log x) ^ (3 : тДЭ) :=
      div_le_div_of_nonneg_left hy_pos.le (rpow_pos_of_pos hlog_pos 3)
        (rpow_le_rpow hlog_pos.le hlog_ge (by grind))
    calc (q : тДЭ) тЙд y + y / (log y) ^ (3 : тДЭ) := hq
      _ тЙд y + y / (log x) ^ (3 : тДЭ) := add_le_add_right h y
      _ = y * (1 + ╬╡) := by simp only [h╬╡_def, тЖР rpow_natCast]; field_simp; ring_nf
  -- Get upper bounds
  have hqтВА_ub' : (qтВА : тДЭ) тЙд n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2 := by
    have := upper (by positivity) hyтВА_ge_x hqтВА_ub
    calc (qтВА : тДЭ) тЙд (n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3) * (1 + ╬╡) := this
      _ = n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2 := by field_simp
  have hqтВБ_ub' : (qтВБ : тДЭ) тЙд n / (1 + ╬╡) := by
    have := upper (by positivity) hyтВБ_ge_x hqтВБ_ub
    calc (qтВБ : тДЭ) тЙд (n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2) * (1 + ╬╡) := this
      _ = n / (1 + ╬╡) := by field_simp
  have hqтВВ_ub' : (qтВВ : тДЭ) тЙд n := by
    have := upper (by positivity) hyтВВ_ge_x hqтВВ_ub
    calc (qтВВ : тДЭ) тЙд (n / (1 + ╬╡)) * (1 + ╬╡) := this
      _ = n := by field_simp
  -- StrictMono: qтВА < qтВБ < qтВВ
  have hqтВА_lt_qтВБ : qтВА < qтВБ := Nat.cast_lt.mp (hqтВА_ub'.trans_lt hqтВБ_lb)
  have hqтВБ_lt_qтВВ : qтВБ < qтВВ := Nat.cast_lt.mp (hqтВБ_ub'.trans_lt hqтВВ_lb)
  -- qтВВ < n: the Dusart interval has upper bound yтВВ * (1 + 1/(log yтВВ)┬│) < yтВВ * (1 + ╬╡) = n
  have hqтВВ_lt_n : qтВВ < n := by
    have hyтВВ_pos : 0 < (n : тДЭ) / (1 + ╬╡) := by positivity
    have hyтВВ_lt_n : n / (1 + ╬╡) < n := div_lt_self hn_pos (by linarith)
    have hlog_yтВВ_pos : 0 < log (n / (1 + ╬╡)) := log_pos (by linarith : 1 < (n : тДЭ) / (1 + ╬╡))
    have hlog_yтВВ_gt : log (n / (1 + ╬╡)) > log x := log_lt_log hx_pos (lt_of_lt_of_le hyтВВ_lt_n le_rfl)
    have hqтВВ_strict : (qтВВ : тДЭ) < n := by
      calc (qтВВ : тДЭ) тЙд n / (1 + ╬╡) + (n / (1 + ╬╡)) / (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ (3 : тДЭ) := hqтВВ_ub
        _ = (n / (1 + ╬╡)) * (1 + 1 / (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ 3) := by field_simp
        _ < (n / (1 + ╬╡)) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) := by
          apply mul_lt_mul_of_pos_left _ hyтВВ_pos
          gcongr
          exact pow_lt_pow_leftтВА hlog_yтВВ_gt.le hlog_pos.le (by norm_num)
        _ = (n / (1 + ╬╡)) * (1 + ╬╡) := by simp only [h╬╡_def]; field_simp
        _ = n := by field_simp
    exact Nat.cast_lt.mp hqтВВ_strict
  refine тЯи![qтВА, qтВБ, qтВВ], fun i тЖж by fin_cases i <;> assumption,
    Fin.strictMono_iff_lt_succ.mpr fun i тЖж by fin_cases i <;> assumption, fun i тЖж ?_, hqтВВ_lt_nтЯй
  fin_cases i <;> simp only [h╬╡_def]
  ┬╖ simp only [CharP.cast_eq_zero, sub_zero]
    calc (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 : тДЭ))

  ┬╖ simp only [Nat.cast_ofNat]
    calc (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 - 2 : тДЭ))
        = n * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-1 : тДЭ) := by ring_nf
      _ = n / (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) := by rw [rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, rpow_one]; field_simp
      _ тЙд qтВВ := hqтВВ_lb.le

blueprint_comment /--
\subsection{Bounding the factors in \eqref{eq:main-ineq}}
-/

@[blueprint
  "lem:qi-product"
  (title := "Bounds for the \\(q_i\\)-product")
  (statement := /--
  With \(p_i,q_i\) as in Lemmas~\ref{lem:choose-pi} and \ref{lem:choose-qi}, we have
  \begin{equation}\label{eq:qi-upper}
    \prod_{i=1}^3 \Bigl(1 + \frac{1}{q_i}\Bigr)
    \le
    \prod_{i=1}^3 \Bigl(1 + \frac{\bigl(1 + \frac{1}{\log^3 \sqrt{n}}\bigr)^i}{n} \Bigr).
  \end{equation}
  -/)
  (proof := /--
  By Lemma~\ref{lem:choose-qi}, each \(q_i\) is at least
  \[

For log_lt_log we need:

...

The error is at line 863. The goal is to show log (n / (1 + ╬╡)) > log x, i.e., log x < log (n / (1 + ╬╡)).

For log_lt_log we need:

  • log_lt_log hx_pos hab where hab : x < n / (1 + ╬╡)

We have hyтВВ_ge_x : n / (1 + ╬╡) тЙе x, so we need the strict version. Since n / (1 + ╬╡) is the y_2 value, and we showed hyтВВ_ge_x, we have x тЙд n / (1 + ╬╡). But to get log x < log (n/(1+╬╡)), we need x < n/(1+╬╡) strictly.

Actually, we can prove x < n / (1 + ╬╡) because:

  • x = тИЪn
  • n / (1 + ╬╡) = n / (1 + 1/(log x)┬│)
  • Since n тЙе XтВА┬▓ тЙе 89693┬▓ is very large and ╬╡ is small, n/(1+╬╡) > x = тИЪn

Actually n/(1+╬╡) тЙе x┬▓/2 тЙе x from hyтВА_ge_x and since n/(1+╬╡) тЙе n/(1+╬╡)┬│, we get n/(1+╬╡) > x strictly because x┬▓ / 2 > x for large enough x.

Actually let's think more carefully:

  • hyтВВ_ge_x : n / (1 + ╬╡) тЙе x - this is weak (тЙе)

  • But n / (1 + ╬╡) тЙе x┬▓ / 2 from earlier work

  • For x тЙе XтВА тЙе 89693, we have x┬▓ / 2 > x since this holds whenever x > 2

  • Therefore n / (1 + ╬╡) > x strictly, giving us the strict inequality needed for log_lt_log leis being applied witha = n / (1 + ╬╡), b = n, and c = n. But that gives us n / (1 + ╬╡) < n`, which is what we already have. We need to chain through the inequalities differently to get the strict inequality we need for the logarithm argument.

The key insight is that we have n / (1 + ╬╡) > n / (1 + ╬╡)┬│ when ╬╡ > 0, combined with n / (1 + ╬╡)┬│ тЙе x┬▓/2 > x. This gives us a strict chain that should work directly with the logarithm lemma. le hyтВВ_lt_n le_rflwould given / (1 + ╬╡) < n` which isn't what we want.

We need x < n / (1 + ╬╡) for log_lt_log hx_pos _.

Let me create a helper hx_lt_yтВВ : x < n / (1 + ╬╡). We have hyтВВ_ge_x : n / (1 + ╬╡) тЙе x which is the weak form.

We can prove the strict form using that n / (1 + ╬╡) тЙе n / (1 + ╬╡)┬│ тЙе x┬▓ / 2 > x (for x > 2).

Actually, simpler: we have hyтВА_ge_x : n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 тЙе x. And n / (1 + ╬╡) > n / (1 + ╬╡)┬│ since (1+╬╡) < (1+╬╡)┬│ when ╬╡ > 0. So the strict version follows.

The error at line 874 is that ring doesn't work for goals like a * a + a = a + a * a. I need to use ring_nf instead (or just note that this is add_comm situation). Actually looking at the diagnostic, the unsolved goal is:

тКв Real.log (тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 3 * Real.log (тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 3 + Real.log (тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 3 =
    Real.log (тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 3 + Real.log (тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 3 * Real.log (тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 3
```This is just `a * a + a = a + a * a` which is true by commutativity of addition. `ring` should work. Let me try `ring_nf` as suggested, or just `ac_rfl` or `ring`.Actually it says `ring_nf` works. Let me use that.

I see - the goal BEFORE ring_nf is:

    Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ 3 * (Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ 3 + 1)```This is `(a + 1) * a = a * (a + 1)` which is `mul_comm`.After `ring_nf`, it becomes:
```Real.log (тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 3 * Real.log (тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 3 + Real.log (тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 3 =
    Real.log (тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 3 + Real.log (тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 3 * Real.log (тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 3```Which is `a┬▓ + a = a + a┬▓`. This needs `add_comm (a * a) a`.Let me just use `mul_comm` on the original goal before `ring_nf`.

Actually, looking at the calc line:calc (qтВВ : тДЭ) тЙд n / (1 + ╬╡) + (n / (1 + ╬╡)) / (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ (3 : тДЭ) := hqтВВ_ub The ^ (3 : тДЭ) is rpow.         _ = (n / (1 + ╬╡)) * (1 + 1 / (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ 3) := by
            have hlog_ne : (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ 3 тЙа 0 := by positivity
            field_simp [hlog_ne, ne_of_gt h1╬╡_pos]; ring_nf
        _ < (n / (1 + ╬╡)) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) := by
          apply mul_lt_mul_of_pos_left _ hyтВВ_pos
          gcongr
          exact pow_lt_pow_leftтВА hlog_yтВВ_gt.le hlog_pos.le (by norm_num)
        _ = (n / (1 + ╬╡)) * (1 + ╬╡) := by simp only [h╬╡_def]
        _ = n := by field_simp
    exact Nat.cast_lt.mp hqтВВ_strict
  refine тЯи![qтВА, qтВБ, qтВВ], fun i тЖж by fin_cases i <;> assumption,
    Fin.strictMono_iff_lt_succ.mpr fun i тЖж by fin_cases i <;> assumption, fun i тЖж ?_, hqтВВ_lt_nтЯй
  fin_cases i <;> simp only [h╬╡_def]
  ┬╖ simp only [CharP.cast_eq_zero, sub_zero]
    calc (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 : тДЭ))
        = n / (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ 3 := by rw [rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, rpow_natCast]; field_simp
      _ тЙд qтВА := hqтВА_lb.le
  ┬╖ simp only [Nat.cast_one]
    calc (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 - 1 : тДЭ))
        = n * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-2 : тДЭ) := by ring_nf
      _ = n / (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ 2 := by rw [rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, rpow_natCast]; field_simp
      _ тЙд qтВБ := hqтВБ_lb.le
  ┬╖ simp only [Nat.cast_ofNat]
    calc (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 - 2 : тДЭ))
        = n * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-1 : тДЭ) := by ring_nf
      _ = n / (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) := by rw [rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, rpow_one]; field_simp
      _ тЙд qтВВ := hqтВВ_lb.le

  2. The rpow_natCast rewrite fails

Looking at the calc lines more carefully, the issue is that the statement uses:
n * (1 + 1 / (log тИЪ(n : тДЭ)) ^ 3) ^ (-((3 : тДЭ) - (i : тДХ)))

After fin_cases i and simp only [h╬╡_def], the expression becomes something like:
(n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 : тДЭ))

But this should still be valid... Let me check what's happening by looking at the goal state at line 888.

Actually, looking at line 887-890, the calc statement starts with:

calc (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 : тДЭ))

But this might have a type issue because of the natural number cast. The expression (n : тДЭ) should be fine, but let me check if there's an issue with how n is being interpreted.

Actually looking at the error more carefully:```
[Line 888:10] failed to synthesize
HMul тДЭ тДЭ тДХ


This suggests that at line 888, there's a multiplication of two reals but the result is expected to be a natural number. This would happen if the calc chain expects a natural number type.


The original statement has `n` as a natural number, and since `q i` is also natural, the comparison `n * ... тЙд q i` needs type consistency. The left side needs to be coerced to тДЭ to match the arithmetic operations, but then we're comparing тДЭ to тДХ which creates the type mismatch.

At line 890, `hqтВА_lb : тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 < тЖСqтВА` is already in тДЭ, so using `hqтВА_lb.le` should work if the calc chain maintains real-valued expressions throughout. The problem seems to be that `fin_cases` combined with `simp` is misinterpreting how the types flow through the expression. I need to make the type coercions explicit in the `fin_cases` section rather than relying on implicit inference.

The calc proof starts with:

But the issue is that after simp only [h╬╡_def], the goal has -(3 - тЖС0) not -(3 : тДЭ). Let me check what happens.

Actually, looking at the goal state:тКв тЖСn * (1 + 1 / Real.log x ^ 3) ^ (-(3 - тЖС0)) тЙд тЖС(![qтВА, qтВБ, qтВВ] тЯи0, тЛптЯй)Here тЖС0 is a natural number cast. So -(3 - тЖС0) = -3. But the calc line has (-(3 : тДЭ)) which should be the same.

Looking at the type error HMul тДЭ тДЭ тДХ at line 889:
The calc line is:calc (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 : тДЭ)) = n / (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ 3 := ...

The problem is that the goal expects тЖСn * ... but the calc starts with (n : тДЭ) *, and somewhere the simplification is causing type confusion where the result is being treated as a natural number instead of a real. The simp only [CharP.cast_eq_zero, sub_zero] on line 888 might be altering the type structure in unexpected ways.

Looking at the case split on i, when i = 0, the goal becomes тЖСn * (1 + 1 / Real.log x ^ 3) ^ (-(3 - тЖС0)) тЙд тЖС(![qтВА, qтВБ, qтВВ] тЯи0, тЛптЯй). The simp lemma CharP.cast_eq_zero is converting тЖС0 : тДЭ to 0, which simplifies 3 - тЖС0 to 3 - 0 = 3, and then the exponent becomes -3. The issue is that this simplification might be changing how Lean interprets the type of the entire expression.

I should try proving each case directly without relying on calc mode, which seems to be introducing complications with how the casts and subtractions interact.

...

Let me check: the calc starts with (n : тДЭ) * ... but the goal has тЖСn * .... These should be the same since тЖСn : тДЭ = (n : тДЭ). But the error HMul тДЭ тДЭ тДХ suggests the calc is trying to produce a natural number.

Looking more carefully, the issue might be that after simp only [h╬╡_def] on the first case, the goal becomes:
тЖСn * (1 + 1 / Real.log x ^ 3) ^ (-(3 - тЖС0)) тЙд тЖС(![qтВА, qтВБ, qтВВ] тЯи0, тЛптЯй)

The RHS тЖС(![qтВА, qтВБ, qтВВ] тЯи0, тЛптЯй) coerces a natural number to something. If this something is not тДЭ, then we have an issue.

Actually, looking at the theorem statement:
(тИА i : Fin 3, n * (1 + 1 / (log тИЪ(n : тДЭ)) ^ 3) ^ (-((3 : тДЭ) - (i : тДХ))) тЙд q i)

Here n and q i are natural numbers, but the LHS involves real arithmetic. The multiplication n * ... where the second operand is real will coerce n to real, making the LHS real-valued. Yet the inequality compares this to q i, which is a natural numberтАФthis type mismatch needs resolution through coercion.

Looking at the full expression: (1 + 1 / (log тИЪ(n : тДЭ)) ^ 3) is real, and raising it to the power (-((3 : тДЭ) - (i : тДХ))) via rpow yields a real. Multiplying n by this real result should produce a real through implicit coercion. The comparison тЙд q i then requires q i to be coerced to real as well, or there's a missing type annotation somewhere in the statement.

The actual error HMul тДЭ тДЭ тДХ suggests the calc block is producing a real-valued expression but the goal expects a natural number, or vice versa. The calc at line 889 is attempting to establish an equality with the goal, but after simplification the goal state may have shifted unexpectedly. Rather than debugging the calc chain further, replacing the entire fin_cases section with a more direct proof that avoids these calc blocks should resolve the type mismatch.

RHS: a * (a + 1) = a┬▓ + aSo actually LHS = RHS by commutativity.

But add_comm only rewrites one side. If I write rw [add_comm], it will rewrite LHS from (1 + a) * a to (a + 1) * a. Let me think about what's happening.

    have hqтВВ_strict : (qтВВ : тДЭ) < n := by
      calc (qтВВ : тДЭ) тЙд n / (1 + ╬╡) + (n / (1 + ╬╡)) / (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ 3 := hqтВВ_ub
        _ = (n / (1 + ╬╡)) * (1 + 1 / (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ 3) := by
            have hlog_ne : (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ 3 тЙа 0 := by positivity
            field_simp [hlog_ne, ne_of_gt h1╬╡_pos]
            rw [add_comm, mul_comm]
        _ < (n / (1 + ╬╡)) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) := by
          apply mul_lt_mul_of_pos_left _ hyтВВ_pos
          gcongr
          exact pow_lt_pow_leftтВА hlog_yтВВ_gt.le hlog_pos.le (by norm_num)
        _ = (n / (1 + ╬╡)) * (1 + ╬╡) := by simp only [h╬╡_def]
        _ = n := by field_simp
    exact Nat.cast_lt.mp hqтВВ_strict
  refine тЯи![qтВА, qтВБ, qтВВ], fun i тЖж by fin_cases i <;> assumption,
    Fin.strictMono_iff_lt_succ.mpr fun i тЖж by fin_cases i <;> assumption, fun i тЖж ?_, hqтВВ_lt_nтЯй
  fin_cases i <;> simp only [h╬╡_def, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one, Matrix.head_cons]
  ┬╖ -- Case i = 0: show n * (1 + ╬╡)^(-3) тЙд qтВА
    simp only [Fin.val_zero, CharP.cast_eq_zero, sub_zero]
    have heq : (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 : тДЭ)) = n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 := by
      rw [rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, rpow_natCast, one_div, mul_inv_eq_div]
    rw [heq]
    exact hqтВА_lb.le
  ┬╖ -- Case i = 1: show n * (1 + ╬╡)^(-2) тЙд qтВБ
    simp only [Fin.val_one, Nat.cast_one]
    have heq : (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 - 1 : тДЭ)) = n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2 := by
      have h1 : -(3 - 1 : тДЭ) = -2 := by ring
      rw [h1, rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, rpow_natCast, one_div, mul_inv_eq_div]
    rw [heq]
    exact hqтВБ_lb.le
  ┬╖ -- Case i = 2: show n * (1 + ╬╡)^(-1) тЙд qтВВ
    simp only [Fin.val_ofNat'_eq, Nat.cast_ofNat]
    have heq : (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 - 2 : тДЭ)) = n / (1 + ╬╡) := by
      have h1 : -(3 - 2 : тДЭ) = -1 := by ring
      rw [h1, rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, rpow_one, one_div, mul_inv_eq_div]
    rw [heq]
    exact hqтВВ_lb.le

      simp only [rpow_natCast]
      ring
    rw [heq]
    exact hqтВБ_lb.le
  ┬╖ -- Case i = 2: show n * (1 + ╬╡)^(-1) тЙд qтВВ
    simp only [Fin.val_ofNat', Nat.cast_ofNat, Matrix.cons_val_one, Matrix.head_cons]
    have heq : (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 - 2 : тДЭ)) = n / (1 + ╬╡) := by
      have h1 : -(3 - 2 : тДЭ) = -1 := by ring
      rw [h1, rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, rpow_one]
      ring
    rw [heq]
    exact hqтВВ_lb.le

blueprint_comment /--
\subsection{Bounding the factors in \eqref{eq:main-ineq}}
-/

This is a * (1 + a) = a * (a + 1). This needs another add_comm to turn (1 + a) into (a + 1). Let me add one more add_comm.

There are still errors at lines 872 and 879. Also there's an info message about using ring_nf instead of ring. Let me check these issues and fix them.

The error at line 872 shows that after rw [add_comm, mul_comm, add_comm, add_comm], the goal is:
Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ 3 * (1 + Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ 3) = Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ 3 * (Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ 3 + 1)This is a * (1 + a) = a * (a + 1). I need one more add_comm for the inner (1 + a) on the LHS.

Actually this should just be rfl after enough add_comms. Let me try congr 1; ring.

After congr 1; ring, we have two goals:1. Real.log (тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 3 = Real.log (тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 3 - trivially true by rfl
2. 1 + Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ 3 = Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ 3 + 1 - add_comm

So I need <;> rfl <|> add_comm or similar. Let me use <;> first | rfl | exact add_comm _ _.

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The conversation is summarized below:
Analysis:
Let me analyze this conversation chronologically to capture all important details.

  1. Initial Context: The user is working on a Lean 4 project called PrimeNumberTheoremAnd, which formalizes the Prime Number Theorem. The conversation was continued from a previous session.

  2. Main Task: The user asked to prove the exists_q_primes theorem in /Users/eric/GitHub/PrimeNumberTheoremAnd/PrimeNumberTheoremAnd/Lcm.lean by clearing the sorry on line 776.

  3. Theorem Statement: exists_q_primes states that for n тЙе XтВА┬▓ (where XтВА = 89693), there exist three primes qтВА < qтВБ < qтВВ < n with specific lower bounds related to n * (1 + 1/(log тИЪn)┬│)^(-(3-i)).

  4. Proof Approach: Uses Dusart's theorem (Dusart.proposition_5_4) which guarantees primes in intervals (y, y + y/(log y)┬│] for y тЙе XтВА.

  5. Multiple Errors Encountered and Fixed:

    • log XтВА ambiguity тЖТ fixed by using log (XтВА : тДЭ) explicitly
    • pow_lt_pow_right doesn't exist тЖТ tried pow_lt_pow_right' but that needed MulLeftStrictMono тДЭ which failed тЖТ fixed with nlinarith [sq_nonneg ╬╡, sq_nonneg (1 + ╬╡)]
    • lt_of_lt_of_le wrong argument order тЖТ fixed by creating explicit hx_lt_yтВВ proof
    • field_simp leaving commutativity goals тЖТ multiple attempts with ring, ring_nf, add_comm, mul_comm, finally using congr 1 approach
    • rpow_natCast rewrite failures тЖТ pattern didn't match because expression had different form
    • simp only [rpow_natCast] made no progress тЖТ rewrote using тЖР rpow_natCast (1 + ╬╡) k; ring
    • Fin.val_ofNat'_eq unknown constant тЖТ changed to Fin.val_ofNat'
    • Type synthesis errors HMul тДЭ тДЭ тДХ тЖТ avoided by restructuring the calc blocks

  6. Current State: The proof is still not complete. The main remaining error is at line 872-875 where the commutativity issue persists. After field_simp, rw [add_comm, mul_comm], and congr 1, there are two goals:

    • Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ (3 : тДХ) = Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ (3 : тДЭ) - definitional equality issue between ^ (3 : тДХ) and ^ (3 : тДЭ)
    • 1 + Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ 3 = Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ 3 + 1 - needs add_comm

  7. User Messages: The user only provided the initial request to continue the task. All other messages were system reminders or tool results.

  8. Important File Sections: The proof is at lines 776-907 of Lcm.lean.

Summary:

  1. Primary Request and Intent:
    The user asked to prove the exists_q_primes theorem in /Users/eric/GitHub/PrimeNumberTheoremAnd/PrimeNumberTheoremAnd/Lcm.lean by clearing the sorry. This is a Lean 4 formalization project for the Prime Number Theorem. The theorem states that for n тЙе XтВА┬▓ (XтВА = 89693), there exist three primes qтВА < qтВБ < qтВВ < n with lower bounds n * (1 + 1/(log тИЪn)┬│)^(-(3-i)) тЙд qс╡в.

  2. Key Technical Concepts:

    • Lean 4 theorem proving
    • Dusart's theorem (Dusart.proposition_5_4): For x тЙе XтВА, there exists prime p with x < p тЙд x + x/(log x)┬│
    • Real number rpow (real exponentiation) vs natural number pow
    • field_simp tactic for algebraic manipulations
    • fin_cases for case analysis on Fin 3
    • Commutativity issues with ring, add_comm, mul_comm
    • Type coercion between тДХ and тДЭ
    • nlinarith for nonlinear arithmetic

  3. Files and Code Sections:

    • /Users/eric/GitHub/PrimeNumberTheoremAnd/PrimeNumberTheoremAnd/Lcm.lean (lines 776-907)
      • Main file containing exists_q_primes theorem
      • Current proof structure (partially complete):
      theorem exists_q_primes {n : тДХ} (hn : n тЙе XтВА ^ 2) :
    тИГ q : Fin 3 тЖТ тДХ, (тИА i, Nat.Prime (q i)) тИз StrictMono q тИз
      (тИА i : Fin 3, n * (1 + 1 / (log тИЪ(n : тДЭ)) ^ 3) ^ (-((3 : тДЭ) - (i : тДХ))) тЙд q i) тИз q 2 < n := by
  let x := тИЪ(n : тДЭ)
  -- ... setup with ╬╡ = 1 / (log x) ^ 3
  -- Apply Dusart to get primes qтВА, qтВБ, qтВВ
  -- Prove ordering and bounds
      • Problematic section at line 872-875:
      _ = (n / (1 + ╬╡)) * (1 + 1 / (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ 3) := by
    have hlog_ne : (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ 3 тЙа 0 := by positivity
    field_simp [hlog_ne, ne_of_gt h1╬╡_pos]
    rw [add_comm, mul_comm]; congr 1 <;> [rfl; exact add_comm _ _]
      • Fin_cases section at lines 885-905:
      fin_cases i <;> simp only [h╬╡_def]
┬╖ -- Case i = 0
  simp only [CharP.cast_eq_zero, sub_zero, Matrix.cons_val_zero]
  have heq : (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 : тДЭ)) = n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 := by
    rw [rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, тЖР rpow_natCast (1 + ╬╡) 3]; ring
  rw [heq]
  exact hqтВА_lb.le

  4. Errors and fixes:

    • log XтВА ambiguity between Real.log and Nat.log: Fixed by using log (XтВА : тДЭ) with explicit cast
    • pow_lt_pow_right unknown identifier: Tried pow_lt_pow_right' but that failed with MulLeftStrictMono тДЭ error. Fixed using nlinarith [sq_nonneg ╬╡, sq_nonneg (1 + ╬╡)]
    • lt_of_lt_of_le hyтВВ_lt_n wrong argument order: Created explicit proof hx_lt_yтВВ : x < n / (1 + ╬╡) using div_lt_div_of_pos_left
    • field_simp leaving commutativity goals: After field_simp, goals like (a + 1) * a = a * (a + 1) remained. Used rw [add_comm, mul_comm]; congr 1 approach
    • rpow_natCast rewrite failures: Pattern ?x ^ тЖС?n didn't match. Fixed by using тЖР rpow_natCast (1 + ╬╡) k to introduce the pattern first
    • simp only [rpow_natCast] made no progress: Removed and restructured proofs
    • Definitional equality ^ (3 : тДХ) vs ^ (3 : тДЭ): After congr 1, the first goal has this mismatch - still unresolved

  5. Problem Solving:

    • Successfully structured proof following exists_p_primes pattern
    • Established ╬╡ = 1/(log x)┬│ where x = тИЪn
    • Proved y_i тЙе XтВА for Dusart application
    • Created upper helper lemma for converting Dusart upper bounds
    • Established strict ordering qтВА < qтВБ < qтВВ
    • Proved qтВВ < n
    • Ongoing issue: The commutativity goal at line 872 after congr 1 has a definitional inequality between ^ (3 : тДХ) and ^ (3 : тДЭ)

  6. All user messages:

    • "This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context..." (with summary and instruction to continue without asking questions)

  7. Pending Tasks:

    • Complete the proof of exists_q_primes by fixing the remaining errors at lines 872-879 and 889/896

  8. Current Work:
    The proof is almost complete but has remaining errors. The main issue is at line 875 where after field_simp [hlog_ne, ne_of_gt h1╬╡_pos] and rw [add_comm, mul_comm]; congr 1 <;> [rfl; exact add_comm _ _], there are errors:

    • rfl fails because Real.log (...) ^ (3 : тДХ) is not definitionally equal to Real.log (...) ^ (3 : тДЭ)
    • add_comm _ _ has type mismatch

    Additionally, lines 889 and 896 have info messages suggesting ring_nf instead of ring.

  9. Optional Next Step:
    Fix the remaining errors in the exists_q_primes proof:

    1. Replace the line 875 tactic with something that handles the ^ (3 : тДХ) vs ^ (3 : тДЭ) mismatch (likely needs norm_cast or explicit rpow_natCast rewrite)
    2. Replace ring with ring_nf at lines 889 and 896 as suggested by the linter
    3. Clean up unused simp arguments in the fin_cases section.

@[blueprint "lem:choose-qi"
  (title := "Choice of large primes \\(q_i\\)")
  (statement := /--
  Let \(n \ge X_0^2\). Then there exist primes \(q_1 < q_2 < q_3\) with
  \[
    q_{4-i} \ge n \Bigl(1 + \frac{1}{\log^3 \sqrt{n}}\Bigr)^{-i}
  \]
  for \(i = 1,2,3\), and \(q_1 < q_2 < q_3 < n\).
  -/)
  (proof := /-- Apply Theorem~\ref{thm:Dusart} with suitable values of \(x\) slightly below \(n\),
  e.g.\ \(x = n(1+1/\log^3\sqrt{n})^{-i}\), again keeping track of the intervals.  For \(n\) large
  enough, these intervals lie in \((\sqrt{n},n)\) and contain primes \(q_i\) with the desired
  ordering. -/)
  (proofUses := ["thm:Dusart"])
  (latexEnv := "lemma")]
theorem exists_q_primes {n : тДХ} (hn : n тЙе XтВА ^ 2) :
    тИГ q : Fin 3 тЖТ тДХ, (тИА i, Nat.Prime (q i)) тИз StrictMono q тИз
      (тИА i : Fin 3, n * (1 + 1 / (log тИЪ(n : тДЭ)) ^ 3) ^ (-((3 : тДЭ) - (i : тДХ))) тЙд q i) тИз q 2 < n := by
  let x := тИЪ(n : тДЭ)
  have hx_ge : x тЙе XтВА := by simpa using sqrt_le_sqrt (by exact_mod_cast hn : (XтВА : тДЭ) ^ 2 тЙд n)
  have hx_pos : 0 < x := (by grind : (0 : тДЭ) < XтВА).trans_le hx_ge
  have hlog_pos : 0 < log x := log_pos ((by grind : (1 : тДЭ) < XтВА).trans_le hx_ge)
  set ╬╡ := 1 / (log x) ^ 3 with h╬╡_def
  have h╬╡_pos : 0 < ╬╡ := by positivity
  have h1╬╡_pos : 0 < 1 + ╬╡ := by linarith
  have hn_pos : (0 : тДЭ) < n := by
    have : (0 : тДЭ) < XтВА ^ 2 := by grind
    linarith [show (XтВА : тДЭ) ^ 2 тЙд n by exact_mod_cast hn]
  have hn_eq_x2 : (n : тДЭ) = x ^ 2 := (sq_sqrt hn_pos.le).symm
  -- Show that ╬╡ is small (╬╡ тЙд 1/11.4┬│)
  have hlog_ge_114 : log x тЙе 11.4 := by
    calc log x тЙе log (XтВА : тДЭ) := log_le_log (by grind : (0 : тДЭ) < XтВА) hx_ge
      _ тЙе 11.4 := log_XтВА_gt.le
  have h╬╡_small : ╬╡ тЙд 1 / 11.4 ^ 3 := by
    simp only [h╬╡_def]
    apply div_le_div_of_nonneg_left (by norm_num : (0 : тДЭ) тЙд 1)
    ┬╖ apply pow_pos; linarith [log_XтВА_gt]
    ┬╖ apply pow_le_pow_leftтВА (by linarith : (0 : тДЭ) тЙд 11.4) hlog_ge_114
  have h1╬╡3_pos : 0 < (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 := by positivity
  have h1╬╡2_pos : 0 < (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2 := by positivity
  have h1╬╡3_le_2 : (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 тЙд 2 := by
    have h1 : (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 тЙд (1 + 1 / 11.4 ^ 3) ^ 3 := by
      apply pow_le_pow_leftтВА (by linarith) (by linarith)
    calc (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 тЙд (1 + 1 / 11.4 ^ 3) ^ 3 := h1
      _ тЙд 2 := by norm_num
  -- Define y_i = n / (1 + ╬╡)^(3-i), and show y_i тЙе XтВА
  have hyтВА_ge : n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 тЙе XтВА := by
    calc n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 = x ^ 2 / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 := by rw [hn_eq_x2]
      _ тЙе x ^ 2 / 2 := div_le_div_of_nonneg_left (sq_nonneg x) (by grind) h1╬╡3_le_2
      _ тЙе XтВА ^ 2 / 2 := by apply div_le_div_of_nonneg_right (sq_le_sq' (by linarith) hx_ge); norm_num
      _ тЙе XтВА := by norm_num
  have h1╬╡2_le_1╬╡3 : (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2 тЙд (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 := by nlinarith [sq_nonneg ╬╡]
  have h1╬╡_le_1╬╡2 : 1 + ╬╡ тЙд (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2 := by nlinarith [sq_nonneg ╬╡]
  have hyтВБ_ge : n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2 тЙе XтВА := le_trans hyтВА_ge
    (div_le_div_of_nonneg_left hn_pos.le h1╬╡2_pos h1╬╡2_le_1╬╡3)
  have hyтВВ_ge : n / (1 + ╬╡) тЙе XтВА := le_trans hyтВБ_ge
    (div_le_div_of_nonneg_left hn_pos.le h1╬╡_pos h1╬╡_le_1╬╡2)
  -- Apply Dusart to get primes
  obtain тЯиqтВА, hqтВА_prime, hqтВА_lb, hqтВА_ubтЯй := Dusart.proposition_5_4 (n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3) hyтВА_ge
  obtain тЯиqтВБ, hqтВБ_prime, hqтВБ_lb, hqтВБ_ubтЯй := Dusart.proposition_5_4 (n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2) hyтВБ_ge
  obtain тЯиqтВВ, hqтВВ_prime, hqтВВ_lb, hqтВВ_ubтЯй := Dusart.proposition_5_4 (n / (1 + ╬╡)) hyтВВ_ge
  -- Show y_i тЙе x (needed for upper bound helper)
  have hx_ge_2 : x тЙе 2 := by linarith [hx_ge, (by grind : (2 : тДЭ) тЙд XтВА)]
  have hyтВА_ge_x : n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 тЙе x := by
    calc n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 = x ^ 2 / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 := by rw [hn_eq_x2]
      _ тЙе x ^ 2 / 2 := div_le_div_of_nonneg_left (sq_nonneg x) (by grind) h1╬╡3_le_2
      _ тЙе x := by rw [ge_iff_le, le_div_iffтВА' (by norm_num : (0 : тДЭ) < 2)]; nlinarith
  have hyтВБ_ge_x : n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2 тЙе x := le_trans hyтВА_ge_x
    (div_le_div_of_nonneg_left hn_pos.le h1╬╡2_pos h1╬╡2_le_1╬╡3)
  have hyтВВ_ge_x : n / (1 + ╬╡) тЙе x := le_trans hyтВБ_ge_x
    (div_le_div_of_nonneg_left hn_pos.le h1╬╡_pos h1╬╡_le_1╬╡2)
  -- Upper bound helper: show q_i upper bound implies q_i тЙд next threshold
  have upper {y : тДЭ} (hy_pos : 0 < y) (hy_ge : y тЙе x) {q : тДХ}
      (hq : (q : тДЭ) тЙд y + y / (log y) ^ (3 : тДЭ)) : (q : тДЭ) тЙд y * (1 + ╬╡) := by
    have hlog_ge : log y тЙе log x := log_le_log hx_pos hy_ge
    have h : y / (log y) ^ (3 : тДЭ) тЙд y / (log x) ^ (3 : тДЭ) :=
      div_le_div_of_nonneg_left hy_pos.le (rpow_pos_of_pos hlog_pos 3)
        (rpow_le_rpow hlog_pos.le hlog_ge (by grind))
    calc (q : тДЭ) тЙд y + y / (log y) ^ (3 : тДЭ) := hq
      _ тЙд y + y / (log x) ^ (3 : тДЭ) := add_le_add_right h y
      _ = y * (1 + ╬╡) := by simp only [h╬╡_def, тЖР rpow_natCast]; field_simp; ring_nf
  -- Get upper bounds
  have hqтВА_ub' : (qтВА : тДЭ) тЙд n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2 := by
    have := upper (by positivity) hyтВА_ge_x hqтВА_ub
    calc (qтВА : тДЭ) тЙд (n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3) * (1 + ╬╡) := this
      _ = n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2 := by field_simp
  have hqтВБ_ub' : (qтВБ : тДЭ) тЙд n / (1 + ╬╡) := by
    have := upper (by positivity) hyтВБ_ge_x hqтВБ_ub
    calc (qтВБ : тДЭ) тЙд (n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2) * (1 + ╬╡) := this
      _ = n / (1 + ╬╡) := by field_simp
  have hqтВВ_ub' : (qтВВ : тДЭ) тЙд n := by
    have := upper (by positivity) hyтВВ_ge_x hqтВВ_ub
    calc (qтВВ : тДЭ) тЙд (n / (1 + ╬╡)) * (1 + ╬╡) := this
      _ = n := by field_simp
  -- StrictMono: qтВА < qтВБ < qтВВ
  have hqтВА_lt_qтВБ : qтВА < qтВБ := Nat.cast_lt.mp (hqтВА_ub'.trans_lt hqтВБ_lb)
  have hqтВБ_lt_qтВВ : qтВБ < qтВВ := Nat.cast_lt.mp (hqтВБ_ub'.trans_lt hqтВВ_lb)
  -- qтВВ < n: the Dusart interval has upper bound yтВВ * (1 + 1/(log yтВВ)┬│) < yтВВ * (1 + ╬╡) = n
  have hqтВВ_lt_n : qтВВ < n := by
    have hyтВВ_pos : 0 < (n : тДЭ) / (1 + ╬╡) := by positivity
    have hyтВВ_lt_n : n / (1 + ╬╡) < n := div_lt_self hn_pos (by linarith)
    have hlog_yтВВ_pos : 0 < log (n / (1 + ╬╡)) := log_pos (by linarith : 1 < (n : тДЭ) / (1 + ╬╡))
    have hx_lt_yтВВ : x < n / (1 + ╬╡) := by
      have h1╬╡_lt_1╬╡3 : (1 + ╬╡) < (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 := by nlinarith [sq_nonneg ╬╡, sq_nonneg (1 + ╬╡)]
      have h1 : n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 < n / (1 + ╬╡) :=
        div_lt_div_of_pos_left hn_pos h1╬╡_pos h1╬╡_lt_1╬╡3
      calc x тЙд n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 := hyтВА_ge_x
        _ < n / (1 + ╬╡) := h1
    have hlog_yтВВ_gt : log (n / (1 + ╬╡)) > log x := log_lt_log hx_pos hx_lt_yтВВ
    have hqтВВ_strict : (qтВВ : тДЭ) < n := by
      calc (qтВВ : тДЭ) тЙд n / (1 + ╬╡) + (n / (1 + ╬╡)) / (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ 3 := hqтВВ_ub
        _ = (n / (1 + ╬╡)) * (1 + 1 / (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ 3) := by
            have hlog_ne : (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ 3 тЙа 0 := by positivity
            field_simp [hlog_ne, ne_of_gt h1╬╡_pos]
            rw [add_comm, mul_comm]; congr 1 <;> [rfl; exact add_comm _ _]
        _ < (n / (1 + ╬╡)) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) := by
          apply mul_lt_mul_of_pos_left _ hyтВВ_pos
          gcongr
          exact pow_lt_pow_leftтВА hlog_yтВВ_gt.le hlog_pos.le (by norm_num)
        _ = (n / (1 + ╬╡)) * (1 + ╬╡) := by simp only [h╬╡_def]
        _ = n := by field_simp
    exact Nat.cast_lt.mp hqтВВ_strict
  refine тЯи![qтВА, qтВБ, qтВВ], fun i тЖж by fin_cases i <;> assumption,
    Fin.strictMono_iff_lt_succ.mpr fun i тЖж by fin_cases i <;> assumption, fun i тЖж ?_, hqтВВ_lt_nтЯй
  fin_cases i <;> simp only [h╬╡_def]
  ┬╖ -- Case i = 0: show n * (1 + ╬╡)^(-3) тЙд qтВА
    simp only [CharP.cast_eq_zero, sub_zero, Matrix.cons_val_zero]
    have heq : (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 : тДЭ)) = n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 := by
      rw [rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, тЖР rpow_natCast (1 + ╬╡) 3]; ring
    rw [heq]
    exact hqтВА_lb.le
  ┬╖ -- Case i = 1: show n * (1 + ╬╡)^(-2) тЙд qтВБ
    simp only [Nat.cast_one, Matrix.cons_val_one, Matrix.head_cons]
    have heq : (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 - 1 : тДЭ)) = n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2 := by
      have h1 : -(3 - 1 : тДЭ) = -2 := by ring
      rw [h1, rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, тЖР rpow_natCast (1 + ╬╡) 2]; ring
    rw [heq]
    exact hqтВБ_lb.le
  ┬╖ -- Case i = 2: show n * (1 + ╬╡)^(-1) тЙд qтВВ
    simp only [Nat.cast_ofNat, Matrix.cons_val_one, Matrix.head_cons]
    have heq : (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 - 2 : тДЭ)) = n / (1 + ╬╡) := by
      have h1 : -(3 - 2 : тДЭ) = -1 := by ring
      rw [h1, rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, rpow_one]; ring
    rw [heq]
    exact hqтВВ_lb.le

blueprint_comment /--
\subsection{Bounding the factors in \eqref{eq:main-ineq}}
-/

@[blueprint
  "lem:qi-product"
  (title := "Bounds for the \\(q_i\\)-product")
  (statement := /--
  With \(p_i,q_i\) as in Lemmas~\ref{lem:choose-pi} and \ref{lem:choose-qi}, we have
  \begin{equation}\label{eq:qi-upper}
    \prod_{i=1}^3 \Bigl(1 + \frac{1}{q_i}\Bigr)
    \le
    \prod_{i=1}^3 \Bigl(1 + \frac{\bigl(1 + \frac{1}{\log^3 \sqrt{n}}\bigr)^i}{n} \Bigr).

I can see the main errors:

  1. At line 875: The problem is that after field_simp and commutativity rewrites, I have goals where ^ (3 : тДХ) is being compared with ^ (3 : тДЭ) which are not definitionally equal.

  2. Lines 879, 889, 896 have follow-up errors.

The key issue is that field_simp is producing expressions with natural number exponents ^ 3 while the original goal uses real exponents ^ (3 : тДЭ).

Looking at the error message:

  • The goal after field_simp is: Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ 3 = Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ 3 (but the first is ^ (3 : тДХ) and the second is ^ (3 : тДЭ))

I need to use norm_cast to handle the тДХ vs тДЭ exponent issue. Let me try a different approach.

Actually, looking at line 841 which works, it uses:

= y * (1 + ╬╡) := by simp only [h╬╡_def, тЖР rpow_natCast]; field_simp; ring_nf

So the pattern uses rpow_natCast BEFORE field_simp. But here I'm doing it differently - I'm trying to prove an equality that involves ^ 3 where the LHS came from one side and RHS from another.

Let me take a different approach for line 872-875. Instead of using field_simp and then trying to fix commutativity, I should use a cleaner approach.

The goal is:

n / (1 + ╬╡) + (n / (1 + ╬╡)) / (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ 3 = (n / (1 + ╬╡)) * (1 + 1 / (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ 3)

This is just algebra. Setting y = n / (1 + ╬╡) and L = (log y) ^ 3, the goal becomes y + y / L = y * (1 + 1 / L), which simplifies to y + y/L = y + y/L. The issue is that field_simp introduces natural number exponents while the original uses real exponents, causing a type mismatch that needs explicit casting to resolve.

number exponent `^ 3`, so both sides use the same power operation. This is straightforward algebraтАФI can expand `(n / (1 + ╬╡)) * (1 + 1 / (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ 3)` to get `n / (1 + ╬╡) + (n / (1 + ╬╡)) / (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ 3`, which matches the left side from `hqтВВ_ub`. A simpler tactic like `field_simp; ring_nf` should handle this without needing the more complex `congr` approach.

The real problem appears when `field_simp` normalizes the expressionтАФit converts exponents inconsistently, leaving one side with `^ (3 : тДХ)` and the other with `^ (3 : тДЭ)`. I can fix this by normalizing the exponents first using `simp only [тЖР rpow_natCast]` or `push_cast` before applying `field_simp; ring`. The core algebraic identity here is that `y + y/L = y * (1 + 1/L)`, which is just distribution in reverse. needs `add_comm`. The first goal is puzzling thoughтАФit shows `^ (3 : тДХ)` on the left but `^ (3 : тДЭ)` on the right, suggesting `field_simp` introduced `rpow` somewhere. I should use `norm_cast` to handle the type mismatch between natural number and real exponents. The lemma `rpow_natCast` converts between `x ^ (тЖСn : тДЭ)` and `x ^ n` for real `x` and natural `n`, which might be what's needed here.

Looking at a similar proof at line 841, it applies `тЖР rpow_natCast` before calling `field_simp`, then uses `ring_nf`. I should try that approach instead of just `field_simp` alone.

Real.log (тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 3 + Real.log (тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 3 * Real.log (тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 3


This is `a^2 + a = a + a^2` where `a = log(...)^3`. The `ring` tactic should definitely handle this...

Actually, wait. The suggestion says `ring_nf` with `[apply]`. Let me try using `ring_nf` instead of `ring`. The `ring` tactic might be failing because it can't close the goal (it only normalizes), whereas `ring_nf` would leave a normalized goal that might then need `rfl`.

But looking at the existing code, it already has `add_comm` applied before `ring`. After `add_comm`, the goal would become:
`a + a*a = a + a*a`
which should be closed by `ring` as `rfl`.


The issue might be in how the terms are structured after `field_simp`. Maybe `add_comm` isn't being applied correctly to the specific subterm, or perhaps I need to use a more direct approach like `nlinarith` or explicit rewrites to handle the polynomial identity.

Let me consider several tactics: `field_simp; ring_nf`, then possibly `linarith` or `nlinarith` to finish. I could also try a direct proof with `show` or just rely on `nlinarith` alone. The core problem is that `a * a + a` and `a + a * a` are the same by commutativity, so after normalizing with `ring_nf`, the goal should become trivial or require just a final commutative step.

Looking at the actual structure, `add_comm` should rewrite `a*a + a` to `a + a*a`, making them identical. But `ring` might be failing because there are multiple additions in the expression and the rewrite is matching unexpectedly. The error message indicates the real issue appears after `field_simp`, where the goal becomes something like `Real.log (тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)...`, suggesting the problem is more complex than simple commutativity.

Let me define `L = Real.log (тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 3` to simplify. The LHS becomes `L * L + L` and the RHS is `L + L * L`. These are structurally the same once I account for commutativity of multiplication.

Applying `add_comm` to swap the top-level addition on the left side gives `L + L * L`, which matches the right side exactly. The challenge is that `ring` might struggle with `L` containing `Real.log`, which isn't polynomial. I should try `ac_rfl` for associative-commutative equality, or use `field_simp` followed by `rw [add_comm]` to handle the reordering directly.

The error indicates `ring` itself failed rather than leaving unsolved goals, which suggests it might not recognize `Real.log` expressions as valid ring elements despite тДЭ being a commutative ring. Using `ring_nf` to normalize without closing could reveal what remains after simplification.

I could also try introducing a local definition for the logarithmic term to simplify the structure `ring` sees. Setting `L := (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ 3` and then applying `field_simp` followed by `ring` might help the tactic handle the expression more cleanly.

Alternatively, I could manually rewrite the commutativity after `field_simp` if needed, though that would be verbose. The most straightforward approach is probably to test `ring_nf` instead of `ring` to see if the normal form tactic handles the simplified goal better.

After add_comm, we went from L*L + L = L + L*L to something different.Wait, I think the issue is that the file state is not what I expected. The diagnostic checker might be stale. Let me re-read the file.

Actually looking at the new goal:(1 + L) * L = L * (L + 1) This is commutativity of multiplication: (1+L)*L = L*(L+1) which is (1+L)*L = L*(1+L) by add_comm, and then mul_comm.So I need to apply both add_comm and mul_comm. Let me update the proof.

Or I could use ring after the add_comm. Let me try rw [add_comm, mul_comm].

Very interesting - ac_rfl is producing an error that says the LHS and RHS are the same but not definitionally equal. This is very strange. The goal says:```(1 + Real.log (тЖСn / (╬╡ + 1)) ^ 3) * Real.log (тЖСn / (╬╡ + 1)) ^ 3 = (1 + Real.log (тЖСn / (╬╡ + 1)) ^ 3) * Real.log (тЖСn / (╬╡ + 1)) ^ 3


This looks like LHS = RHS exactly! But `rfl` fails. The issue might be subtle - perhaps there's some difference in how `╬╡` appears on one side vs the other, or there's a definitional issue with how expressions are parsed.


...

Very interesting - ac_rfl is producing an error that says the LHS and RHS are the same but not definitionally equal. This is very strange. The goal says:```(1 + Real.log (тЖСn / (╬╡ + 1)) ^ 3) * Real.log (тЖСn / (╬╡ + 1)) ^ 3 = (1 + Real.log (тЖСn / (╬╡ + 1)) ^ 3) * Real.log (тЖСn / (╬╡ + 1)) ^ 3


This looks like LHS = RHS exactly! But `rfl` fails. The issue might be subtle - perhaps there's some difference in how `╬╡` appears on one side vs the other, or there's a definitional issue with how expressions are parsed.

Actually wait, looking more carefully:
- One side has `(╬╡ + 1)` 
- The original expression had `(1 + ╬╡)`These are not definitionally equal! The `field_simp` must have changed `1 + ╬╡` to `╬╡ + 1` on one side but not the other.

Let me look at the original calc step again. The original expression has `log (n / (1 + ╬╡))` but after `field_simp`, it becomes `log (n / (╬╡ + 1))`. But on the other hand, we have `hlog_ne` which is about `log (n / (1 + ╬╡))`.

Actually, looking at the context: `hlog_ne : Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ 3 тЙа 0`
And the goal has: `Real.log (тЖСn / (╬╡ + 1)) ^ 3`These differ by `1 + ╬╡` vs `╬╡ + 1`. The `field_simp` must be normalizing `1 + ╬╡` to `╬╡ + 1` alphabetically or by some order.


The issue is that `field_simp` is rewriting the expressions in different ways on each side. Rather than fighting with the simplifier, I should try computing the equality directly. Setting `y = n / (1 + ╬╡)` and `L = log y` lets me rewrite the goal as showing `y + y / L^3 = y * (1 + 1 / L^3)`, which is just factoring out `y` from both terms.

The algebraic manipulation is simple: expanding the right side gives `y + y / L^3`, which matches the left side exactly. So I can use a calc block with substitutions and let `field_simp` handle the division clearing, followed by `ring` to finish the polynomial algebra. The key is establishing that `L тЙа 0` upfront using `positivity` to avoid division by zero issues.

However, `field_simp` might be reordering terms like `╬╡ + 1` versus `1 + ╬╡`, causing the goal to fail even though both sides are algebraically identical. I should try normalizing the commutativity explicitly with `simp only [add_comm]` after simplification, or use direct rewrites with `mul_add` and `mul_div_assoc` to avoid the simplifier altogether. The real problem seems to be that both sides contain `(╬╡ + 1)` but in different normalized forms after `field_simp` processes them. is obscured in the error output. The goal is to show: n / (1 + ╬╡) + (n / (1 + ╬╡)) / (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ 3 = (n / (1 + ╬╡)) * (1 + 1 / (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ 3)

The simp lemmas should transform the RHS:- mul_add: a * (b + c) = a * b + a * c - so RHS becomes (n/(1+╬╡)) * 1 + (n/(1+╬╡)) * (1/(log...)^3)- mul_one: a * 1 = a - so first term becomes n/(1+╬╡)- mul_one_div: a * (1/b) = a/b - so second term becomes `(n/(1+╬╡))/(log

...)^3`

After these transformations, RHS should equal LHS. But the error shows that after simp, the goal is still LHS = LHS. This suggests simp is applying lemmas to both sides of the equation, which would only happen if the configuration is off. If both sides become identical after simp, then rfl should close it, but it's not workingтАФwhich indicates there might be a deeper issue with how the goal is being processed.

тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡) + тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡) / Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ 3 = тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡) * (1 + 1 / Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ 3)

goals_after (inside conv_rhs):

| тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡) + тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡) / Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ 3

So the conv_rhs successfully transformed the RHS to match the LHS! After the conv block, the goal should be:

тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡) + тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡) / Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ 3 = тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡) + тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡) / Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ 3

This should close with rfl. But the diagnostic check shows there's still an error. The goals_after shows | ... which is a conv mode goal (the | indicates we're in conv mode, targeting an expression).

Wait - that's the issue! The goals_after shows we're still inside the conv mode (indicated by |). The rw inside conv_rhs is transforming the expression but not completing the conv. The conv block should close automatically when the tactic inside completes.

Actually, looking more carefully - after the conv_rhs => rw [...], the main goal should become LHS = RHS where RHS is now equal to LHS. But the goals_after showing | ... suggests we're still in conv mode.

The goals field being null indicates there are no unsolved goals at line 873, so the tactic actually succeeded. I need to check what's happening on the next line to find where the real issue is.

    have hyтВВ_pos : 0 < (n : тДЭ) / (1 + ╬╡) := by positivity
    have hyтВВ_lt_n : n / (1 + ╬╡) < n := div_lt_self hn_pos (by linarith)
    have hlog_yтВВ_pos : 0 < log (n / (1 + ╬╡)) := log_pos (by linarith : 1 < (n : тДЭ) / (1 + ╬╡))
    have hx_lt_yтВВ : x < n / (1 + ╬╡) := by
      have h1╬╡_lt_1╬╡3 : (1 + ╬╡) < (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 := by nlinarith [sq_nonneg ╬╡, sq_nonneg (1 + ╬╡)]
      have h1 : n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 < n / (1 + ╬╡) :=
        div_lt_div_of_pos_left hn_pos h1╬╡_pos h1╬╡_lt_1╬╡3
      calc x тЙд n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 := hyтВА_ge_x
        _ < n / (1 + ╬╡) := h1
    have hlog_yтВВ_gt : log (n / (1 + ╬╡)) > log x := log_lt_log hx_pos hx_lt_yтВВ
    have hqтВВ_strict : (qтВВ : тДЭ) < n := by
      calc (qтВВ : тДЭ) тЙд n / (1 + ╬╡) + (n / (1 + ╬╡)) / (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ 3 := hqтВВ_ub
        _ = (n / (1 + ╬╡)) * (1 + 1 / (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ 3) := by
            conv_rhs => rw [mul_add, mul_one, mul_one_div]
        _ < (n / (1 + ╬╡)) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) := by
          apply mul_lt_mul_of_pos_left _ hyтВВ_pos
          gcongr
          exact pow_lt_pow_leftтВА hlog_yтВВ_gt.le hlog_pos.le (by norm_num)
        _ = (n / (1 + ╬╡)) * (1 + ╬╡) := by simp only [h╬╡_def]
        _ = n := by field_simp
    exact Nat.cast_lt.mp hqтВВ_strict
  refine тЯи![qтВА, qтВБ, qтВВ], fun i тЖж by fin_cases i <;> assumption,
    Fin.strictMono_iff_lt_succ.mpr fun i тЖж by fin_cases i <;> assumption, fun i тЖж ?_, hqтВВ_lt_nтЯй
  fin_cases i <;> simp only [h╬╡_def]
  ┬╖ -- Case i = 0: show n * (1 + ╬╡)^(-3) тЙд qтВА
    simp only [CharP.cast_eq_zero, sub_zero, Matrix.cons_val_zero]
    have heq : (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 : тДЭ)) = n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 := by
      rw [rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, тЖР rpow_natCast (1 + ╬╡) 3]; ring
    rw [heq]
    exact hqтВА_lb.le
  ┬╖ -- Case i = 1: show n * (1 + ╬╡)^(-2) тЙд qтВБ
    simp only [Nat.cast_one, Matrix.cons_val_one, Matrix.head_cons]
    have heq : (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 - 1 : тДЭ)) = n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2 := by
      have h1 : -(3 - 1 : тДЭ) = -2 := by ring
      rw [h1, rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, тЖР rpow_natCast (1 + ╬╡) 2]; ring
    rw [heq]
    exact hqтВБ_lb.le
  ┬╖ -- Case i = 2: show n * (1 + ╬╡)^(-1) тЙд qтВВ
    simp only [Nat.cast_ofNat, Matrix.cons_val_one, Matrix.head_cons]
    have heq : (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 - 2 : тДЭ)) = n / (1 + ╬╡) := by
      have h1 : -(3 - 2 : тДЭ) = -1 := by ring
      rw [h1, rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, rpow_one]; ring
    rw [heq]
    exact hqтВВ_lb.le

The equation becomes y + y/L = y * (1 + 1/L), which expands to y + y/L on both sidesтАФa straightforward ring application. I need to first establish that (log (n / (1 + ╬╡))) ^ 3 is nonzero.

After applying rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, the goal becomes тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣ = тЖСn / (1 + 1 / Real.log x ^ 3) ^ 3, which requires a different approach than the current tactic.

The core issue is that (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 uses a natural number exponent rather than a real exponent. The rpow_natCast lemma converts between x ^ (n : тДЭ) and x ^ n, but the pattern matching fails because the expression doesn't align with what the tactic expects to rewrite.

After applying rpow_neg, I get ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣ where the exponentiation is still over naturals. To use rpow_natCast effectively, I need the expression in the form x ^ n so it can be rewritten to x ^ (n : тДЭ), but the current structure doesn't match this pattern. The real goal is to transform ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣ into (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬│ using properties of exponents and inverses. тДЭ))`

  • RHS: n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3

After applying rpow_neg, the goal becomes (n : тДЭ) * ((1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (3 : тДЭ))тБ╗┬╣ = n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3. The ring tactic should handle this once I establish that 1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3 = 1 + ╬╡. Then I can rewrite the equality and apply the lower bound. rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le transforms (1 + ╬╡) ^ (-(3 : тДЭ)) into ((1 + ╬╡) ^ (3 : тДЭ))тБ╗┬╣. Since ╬╡ = 1 / (log x) ^ 3, this rewrites the LHS to match the form on the right side. The goal becomes showing n * ((1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣ = n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3.

Attempting тЖР rpow_natCast (1 + ╬╡) 3 to convert (1 + ╬╡) ^ (3 : тДЭ) back to (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 fails because the pattern expects ?x ^ (тЖС?n : тДЭ) but the target has тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣ where the exponent is already a natural number, not a cast.

Looking at the actual error and the subsequent goal state, I see that rpow_neg transformed the real exponent ^ (3 : тДЭ) into a natural number exponent ^ 3. This conversion happens because for positive bases with natural exponents, rpow simplifies to pow. The goal now contains (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 and (1 + 1 / Real.log x ^ 3) ^ 3 on both sides, both using natural number exponents rather than real ones.

The remaining step is straightforwardтАФconverting n * (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3тБ╗┬╣ to n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 using the relationship between multiplication by inverses and division. I can apply div_eq_mul_inv.symm to complete this transformation, then verify the inequality holds.

The third case (line 899-901) uses rpow_one and ring. Let me check if this has errors. The IDE showed an error at line 899 about Real.log x ^ 3 + 1 = (1 + ╬╡) * Real.log x ^ 3.

Looking at the code:lean have heq : (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 - 2 : тДЭ)) = n / (1 + ╬╡) := by have h1 : -(3 - 2 : тДЭ) = -1 := by ring rw [h1, rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, rpow_one]; ring

After rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le:- (1 + ╬╡) ^ (-1 : тДЭ) becomes ((1 + ╬╡) ^ (1 : тДЭ))тБ╗┬╣

After rpow_one:

  • ((1 + ╬╡) ^ (1 : тДЭ))тБ╗┬╣ should become (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣

So the goal would be:n * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣ = n / (1 + ╬╡)

This is just mul_inv_eq_div or div_eq_mul_inv.symm. The ring tactic might not handle this.Let me fix this:```lean
have heq : (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 - 2 : тДЭ)) = n / (1 + ╬╡) := by
have h1 : -(3 - 2 : тДЭ) = -1 := by ring
rw [h1, rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, rpow_one, div_eq_mul_inv]


This rewrites the division to multiplication by the inverse, which should make the equality straightforward.

  ┬╖ -- Case i = 0: show n * (1 + ╬╡)^(-3) тЙд qтВА
    simp only [CharP.cast_eq_zero, sub_zero, Matrix.cons_val_zero]
    have heq : (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 : тДЭ)) = n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 := by
      rw [rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, div_eq_mul_inv]
    rw [heq]
    exact hqтВА_lb.le
  ┬╖ -- Case i = 1: show n * (1 + ╬╡)^(-2) тЙд qтВБ
    simp only [Nat.cast_one, Matrix.cons_val_one, Matrix.head_cons]
    have heq : (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 - 1 : тДЭ)) = n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2 := by
      have h1 : -(3 - 1 : тДЭ) = -2 := by ring
      rw [h1, rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, div_eq_mul_inv]
    rw [heq]
    exact hqтВБ_lb.le
  ┬╖ -- Case i = 2: show n * (1 + ╬╡)^(-1) тЙд qтВВ
    simp only [Nat.cast_ofNat, Matrix.cons_val_one, Matrix.head_cons]
    have heq : (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 - 2 : тДЭ)) = n / (1 + ╬╡) := by
      have h1 : -(3 - 2 : тДЭ) = -1 := by ring
      rw [h1, rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, rpow_one, div_eq_mul_inv]
    rw [heq]
    exact hqтВВ_lb.le

I see the errors are persisting. The key errors are:

  1. Line 871: Goal X = X where X = тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡) + тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡) / Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ 3

...

I see the errors are persisting. The key errors are:

  1. Line 871: Goal X = X where X = тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡) + тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡) / Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ 3
  2. Line 885: Goal тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣ = тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣
  3. Line 891: Goal тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 2)тБ╗┬╣ = тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 2)тБ╗┬╣

These goals all look like X = X but are not being closed. This is a definitional equality issue.Wait, I notice something. At line 885:

  • The goal is тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣ = тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣

But the original goal before rpow_neg should have been:

  • тЖСn * (1 + 1 / Real.log x ^ 3) ^ (-3) = тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3

After rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, it should be:

  • тЖСn * ((1 + 1 / Real.log x ^ 3) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣ = тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3

Note that on the LHS, it's (1 + 1 / Real.log x ^ 3) ^ 3 but on the goal it shows (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3. Since ╬╡ = 1 / Real.log x ^ 3, these should be the same.

When I apply div_eq_mul_inv, the RHS тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 becomes тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣. After both transformations, both sides should be тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣, but they're not being recognized as definitionally equal.

The issue is that ╬╡ gets expanded to 1 / Real.log x ^ 3 on one side while remaining as ╬╡ on the other. I should try simp only [h╬╡_def] to unfold the definition consistently across both sides, or add rfl after the rewrites. Looking at the goal output though, both sides show ╬╡ rather than the expanded form, so they should matchтАФunless Lean's internal representation is causing the mismatch. In Lean 4, there might be subtleties in how these expressions are being handled that prevent definitional equality from working as expected.

have heq : (n : тДЭ) * (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-(3 - 1 : тДЭ)) = n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 2 := by
have h1 : -(3 - 1 : тДЭ) = -2 := by ring
- rw [h1, rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, div_eq_mul_inv]
+ simp only [h1, h╬╡_def, rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, div_eq_mul_inv]

  1. Line 885: Goal тЖСn * (1 + 1 * (Real.log x ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣) ^ (-3) = тЖСn * ((1 + 1 * (Real.log x ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣

Looking at the errors now:

  1. Line 885: Goal тЖСn * (1 + 1 * (Real.log x ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣) ^ (-3) = тЖСn * ((1 + 1 * (Real.log x ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣

    • This is n * base^(-3) = n * (base^3)^(-1) which needs rpow_neg

  2. Line 891: Goal тЖСn * (1 + 1 * (Real.log x ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣) ^ (-2) = тЖСn * ((1 + 1 * (Real.log x ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 2)тБ╗┬╣

    • Same issue

  3. Line 898: Goal тЖСn * (1 + 1 * (Real.log x ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣) ^ (-1) = тЖСn * (1 + 1 * (Real.log x ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣)тБ╗┬╣

    • This is n * base^(-1) = n * base^(-1) which should just need rpow_neg_one

The issue is that simp with h╬╡_def is unfolding ╬╡ but rpow_neg is still needed. The simp is saying rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le is unused, which means simp isn't applying that lemma.

The problem is rpow_neg requires a specific form. Let me check its signature:
rpow_neg : тИА (h : 0 тЙд x), x ^ (-y) = (x ^ y)тБ╗┬╣

So for base ^ (-3) to become (base ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣, we need rpow_neg. But simp isn't finding it because the expression might not match the expected pattern.

I should try rw directly instead of relying on simp only. The goal structure shows rpow with negative exponents on the left that need to transform to inverted powers on the rightтАФrpow_neg should handle this, but simp may not be recognizing the pattern correctly.

Analysis:
Let me analyze this conversation chronologically:

  1. Initial Context: The conversation was continued from a previous session that ran out of context. The user was working on proving the exists_q_primes theorem in a Lean 4 project (PrimeNumberTheoremAnd) which formalizes the Prime Number Theorem.

  2. Main Task: Prove exists_q_primes theorem in /Users/eric/GitHub/PrimeNumberTheoremAnd/PrimeNumberTheoremAnd/Lcm.lean by clearing the sorry.

  3. Key Technical Details:

    • The theorem states that for n тЙе XтВА┬▓ (XтВА = 89693), there exist three primes qтВА < qтВБ < qтВВ < n with specific lower bounds
    • Uses Dusart's theorem (Dusart.proposition_5_4) for finding primes in intervals
    • Involves real number exponentiation (rpow), logarithms, and field simplification

  4. Major Errors Encountered and Fixes:

    • field_simp producing goals like X = X that won't close with rfl due to definitional inequality
    • ring tactic failing with commutativity issues
    • rpow_natCast rewrite failures due to pattern mismatches
    • Various approaches tried: ac_rfl, conv_rhs, ring_nf, manual rewrites

  5. Current Work: The assistant was fixing the fin_cases section (lines 886-903) where proofs need to show equalities involving rpow_neg. The latest fix changed from simp only [h╬╡_def, rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, div_eq_mul_inv] to using rw followed by simp only [rpow_natCast].

    • The approach of using rw with rpow_natCast was just started on line 886-888

Summary:

  1. Primary Request and Intent:
    The user asked to prove the exists_q_primes theorem in /Users/eric/GitHub/PrimeNumberTheoremAnd/PrimeNumberTheoremAnd/Lcm.lean by clearing the sorry. This theorem states that for n тЙе XтВА┬▓ (XтВА = 89693), there exist three primes qтВА < qтВБ < qтВВ < n with lower bounds n * (1 + 1/(log тИЪn)┬│)^(-(3-i)) тЙд qс╡в. The proof uses Dusart's theorem to find primes in specific intervals.

  2. Key Technical Concepts:

    • Lean 4 theorem proving
    • Dusart's theorem (Dusart.proposition_5_4): For x тЙе XтВА, there exists prime p with x < p тЙд x + x/(log x)┬│
    • Real exponentiation rpow vs natural number pow
    • field_simp, ring, ring_nf tactics
    • rpow_neg, rpow_natCast, div_eq_mul_inv lemmas
    • conv_rhs for targeting rewrites
    • fin_cases for case analysis on Fin 3
    • Definitional equality issues in Lean 4

  3. Files and Code Sections:

    • /Users/eric/GitHub/PrimeNumberTheoremAnd/PrimeNumberTheoremAnd/Lcm.lean (lines 776-903)
      • Main file containing exists_q_primes theorem
      • Line 872-873 (calc step for hqтВВ_strict): Currently using conv_rhs => rw [mul_add, mul_one, mul_one_div]

  rw [h╬╡_def, rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, div_eq_mul_inv]
  simp only [rpow_natCast]
      • Lines 892-894 (Case i=1): Still needs similar fix
      • Lines 899-901 (Case i=2): Still needs similar fix

  4. Errors and fixes:

    • field_simp leaving goals like X = X that won't close:
      • Tried ring, ring_nf, ac_rfl, add_comm, mul_comm
      • Fixed line 872-873 with conv_rhs => rw [mul_add, mul_one, mul_one_div]
    • rpow_natCast rewrite failures:
      • Pattern ?x ^ тЖС?n didn't match because expressions used different forms

    • Definitional inequality with ╬╡ expansion:
      • simp only [h╬╡_def] was used to unfold ╬╡ = 1 / (log x) ^ 3
      • Issues with let bindings not being consistently expanded
    • Current remaining goals (line 885, 891, 898):
      • тЖСn * base ^ (-3) = тЖСn * (base ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣ - needs rpow_neg applied correctly

  5. Problem Solving:

    • Successfully structured proof following exists_p_primes pattern
    • Established ╬╡ = 1/(log x)┬│ where x = тИЪn
    • Created upper helper lemma for converting Dusart upper bounds
    • Established strict ordering qтВА < qтВБ < qтВВ
    • Proved qтВВ < n (hqтВВ_lt_n)
    • Ongoing: The fin_cases section (lines 884-903) has persistent errors with rpow_neg transformations not being applied correctly by simp

  6. All user messages:

    • Initial continuation message with summary from previous session asking to continue without questions
    • "lets not use that command for timeout reasons" (referring to lean_build)

Now I see the real issue. The error message says:

The left-hand side
  тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ (3 : тДЭ))тБ╗┬╣

...

Now I see the real issue. The error message says:

The left-hand side
  тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ (3 : тДЭ))тБ╗┬╣
is not definitionally equal to the right-hand side
  тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ (3 : тДХ))тБ╗┬╣

So the issue is:

  • LHS has (1 + ╬╡) ^ (3 : тДЭ) (real power via rpow)
  • RHS has (1 + ╬╡) ^ (3 : тДХ) (natural number power via pow)

These are not definitionally equal, but they are propositionally equal via rpow_natCast: x ^ (n : тДЭ) = x ^ n for natural number n.

So I need to use rpow_natCast to convert between these. Let me use simp only [rpow_natCast] or rw [rpow_natCast].

  • RHS: ((1 + ╬╡) ^ (3 : тДХ))тБ╗┬╣ (natural number power via pow)

I need rpow_natCast to convert between these.

The issue is that after rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, the goal is:

тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣ = тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣

...

The issue is that after rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le, the goal is:

тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣ = тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣

Both sides have (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 but the ^ 3 might be different operators (one is rpow with (3 : тДЭ), other is pow with (3 : тДХ)). The rpow_natCast pattern ?x ^ тЖС?n is looking for a coercion that's not there because the 3 is already a natural number literal on the RHS.

Let me look at rpow_natCast signature:rpow_natCast (x : тДЭ) (n : тДХ) : x ^ (n : тДЭ) = x ^ n So rpow_natCast turns x ^ (n : тДЭ) into x ^ n where the latter uses natural number pow.

The goal after rpow_neg has:

  • LHS: uses rpow with exponent (3 : тДЭ) -> (1 + ╬╡) ^ (3 : тДЭ)- RHS: uses pow with exponent (3 : тДХ) -> (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3

So rpow_natCast can rewrite the LHS to match the RHS. But the error says it couldn't find ?x ^ тЖС?n pattern. This suggests the LHS might already be using pow not rpow, or the coercion is displayed differently.

Let me check the goal more carefully. The goal display shows:

тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣ = тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣

This might be misleading - it looks identical but internally they're different. Let me try using тЖР rpow_natCast instead to convert the RHS from pow back to rpow form, which might help unify the expressions.

Wait, that doesn't make sense. Using тЖР rpow_natCast and then simp only [rpow_natCast] would just undo the change. Let me think about this differently.

Actually, looking at the goal after line 887:

  • Before тЖР rpow_natCast: тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣ = тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣ where both sides look identical
  • After тЖР rpow_natCast: тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣ = тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ тЖС3)тБ╗┬╣

...

Wait, that doesn't make sense. Using тЖР rpow_natCast and then simp only [rpow_natCast] would just undo the change. Let me think about this differently.

Actually, looking at the goal after line 887:

  • Before тЖР rpow_natCast: тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣ = тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣ where both sides look identical
  • After тЖР rpow_natCast: тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣ = тЖСn * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ тЖС3)тБ╗┬╣

So тЖР rpow_natCast changed the RHS's ^ 3 (pow) to ^ тЖС3 (rpow). That's the wrong direction. The issue is that after rpow_neg, the LHS has rpow and the RHS has pow.

Let me look at this more carefully. After rpow_neg h1╬╡_pos.le:

  • LHS (1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ (-3) becomes ((1 + 1 / (log x) ^ 3) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣
  • But wait, the ^ 3 on the LHS should be rpow with (3 : тДЭ) since rpow_neg : x ^ (-y) = (x ^ y)тБ╗┬╣

So after rpow_neg, we should have ((1 + ╬╡) ^ (3 : тДЭ))тБ╗┬╣ on the LHS.
The RHS n / (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 after div_eq_mul_inv becomes n * ((1 + ╬╡) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣ where ^ 3 is natural number power.

The real problem is the mismatch: LHS has (1 + ╬╡) ^ (3 : тДЭ) (rpow) while RHS has (1 + ╬╡) ^ 3 (pow). I need rpow_natCast to convert the LHS's rpow to match the RHS's natural number power.

Looking at rpow_natCast, it states x ^ (n : тДЭ) = x ^ n, which is exactly the conversion needed. After applying rpow_neg, the LHS becomes ((1 + ╬╡) ^ (3 : тДЭ))тБ╗┬╣, but they're not definitionally equal because one uses rpow and the other uses pow, even though they display identically. The solution is to apply rpow_natCast to unify the power operations before attempting rfl.

The error shows that ring doesn't work because of a power issue:тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣ + тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣ * (Real.log (тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣ = тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣ + тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣ * (Real.log (тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣))тБ╗┬╣ ^ 3 LHS has (log ... ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣ while RHS has (log ...)тБ╗┬╣ ^ 3. These are actually the same:
(x ^ 3)тБ╗┬╣ = xтБ╗┬╣ ^ 3 = 1/x^3So we need inv_pow or similar to close this. Let me use field_simp which should handle this.

Same issue. The goal is a*a + a = a + a*a where both a terms are log(n * (1+╬╡)тБ╗┬╣) ^ 3. This is commutative. Let me look at it more closely:- LHS: log(...) ^ 3 * log(...) ^ 3 + log(...) ^ 3- RHS: log(...) ^ 3 + log(...) ^ 3 * log(...) ^ 3Let's call y = log(...) ^ 3. Then:- LHS: y * y + y = y┬▓ + y- RHS: y + y * y = y + y┬▓These are equal by commutativity of addition. The fact that ring isn't working suggests there's a definitional inequality between the representations of the log expressions on LHS vs RHS.Let me check whether the expressions тЖСn * (1 + ╬╡)тБ╗┬╣ and тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡) are being treated differently. Now I can see the real issue!The left-hand side Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ (3 : тДХ) * (Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ 3 + 1)is not definitionally equal to the right-hand side Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ (3 : тДЭ) * (Real.log (тЖСn / (1 + ╬╡)) ^ 3 + 1)The LHS has ^ (3 : тДХ) (natural number power, i.e., pow) and the RHS has ^ (3 : тДЭ) (real power, i.e., rpow). This is the exact same issue we saw before!So I need to use rpow_natCast to convert between them. Let me use simp only [rpow_natCast] after mul_comm.

2026-01-10 21:33:57

The exists_q_primes theorem is now complete without any sorry statements. The file outline shows it as a proper theorem at lines 761-907.

Summary of what was fixed:

  1. Fixed the hqтВВ_strict calculation step (line 872-876) by using field_simp [hpos.ne'] with mul_comm and norm_cast to handle the equality between x + x/y┬│ and x * (1 + 1/y┬│)
  2. Fixed the gcongr step (line 877-879) which closed all goals, so removed the unnecessary exact pow_lt_pow_leftтВА... line
  3. Fixed the three fin_cases branches (i=0, i=1, i=2) by using rpow_neg followed by norm_cast to handle the conversion between x ^ (-(k : тДЭ)) and (x ^ k)тБ╗┬╣, and between pow (natural exponent) and rpow (real exponent)
  4. Cleaned up unused simp arguments in each case

The theorem now proves that for n тЙе XтВА┬▓ (where XтВА = 89693), there exist three primes qтВА < qтВБ < qтВВ < n with the lower bound n * (1 + 1/(log тИЪn)┬│)^(-(3-i)) тЙд qс╡в for each i тИИ Fin 3.
